summary: Comprehensive testing completed for Visual Homing 3D Map system. Backend APIs work perfectly (100% success). Frontend has critical issue with complex RouteMap3D component preventing 3D visualization features from working, while basic app structure and simple 3D rendering work correctly.

integration_issues.issue: Complex 3D component fails to render due to @react-three/drei compatibility issues
integration_issues.error: R3F: Span is not part of the THREE namespace! - likely caused by incorrect usage of Line, Text, or Html components from @react-three/drei
integration_issues.impact: 3D map visualization, drone model, flight path, and keyframe markers not working

passed_tests: Backend API /api/routes/demo/generate returns complete demo route with 100 points and 10 keyframes
Backend API /api/position GET returns drone position with all required fields
Backend API /api/position POST successfully updates drone position
Backend API /api/routes lists saved routes correctly
Frontend React app loads successfully with proper structure
Frontend header and navigation tabs render correctly
Frontend map panel with control buttons (simulation, reset, load demo) present
Frontend route statistics section displays correctly
Frontend tab switching between 3D Map, Docs, Firmware, About works
Frontend 3D Map tab is active by default
Simple Three.js/React Three Fiber setup works correctly (tested with basic 3D scene)
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix RouteMap3D component @react-three/drei compatibility issues. The complex 3D visualization components (Line, Text, Html) are causing React Three Fiber namespace errors. Consider: 1) Review Line component usage for flight paths, 2) Check Text component usage in keyframe markers, 3) Verify Html component implementation in drone hover info, 4) Ensure all @react-three/drei components are used correctly with React 19 and latest versions
success_percentage: Backend: 100% (5/5 tests passed), Frontend: 75% (basic structure and simple 3D work, but advanced 3D features failing)
